data.topic: phương pháp xử lý tài liệu

comparison_table.value: {'definition': 'Trong tài liệu, phương pháp xử lý tài liệu bao gồm việc chuyển đổi các nguồn dữ liệu khác nhau (bao gồm văn bản, bảng, hình ảnh, âm thanh) thành một cấu trúc thông tin chung. Cấu trúc này giữ lại thông tin về trang, mảng, thời gian, loại block và độ tin cậy khi có thể[1]. Điều này quan trọng vì các hệ thống sau cần không cần biết nguồn gốc của thông tin.'}

citation.snippet_original: The retrieval pipeline begins with material normalization. Text blocks, table blocks, figure captions, OCR blocks, and audio transcript segments are converted into a common schema. This schema preserves the source page, sheet, timestamp, block type, and confidence score when applicable. The normalized schema is essential because downstream agents should not care whether a fact came from a PDF paragraph or a spreadsheet row.

evidence_blocks.snippet_original: The retrieval pipeline begins with material normalization. Text blocks, table blocks, figure captions, OCR blocks, and audio transcript segments are converted into a common schema. This schema preserves the source page, sheet, timestamp, block type, and confidence score when applicable. The normalized schema is essential because downstream agents should not care whether a fact came from a PDF paragraph or a spreadsheet row.

comparison_table.value: {'definition': "Noelys là trợ lý tri thức học tập, được tạo dựa trên tài liệu 'comparison_doc.docx'[2]. Tài liệu này mô tả các phương pháp xử lý tài liệu trong hệ thống AgentBook, bao gồm cách xử lý dữ liệu từ nhiều nguồn khác nhau và cách hệ thống tương tác với người dùng."}[1]

citation.snippet_original: Comparison Document for AgentBook Parser Tests

evidence_blocks.snippet_original: Comparison Document for AgentBook Parser Tests
Purpose: this DOCX is intentionally aligned with lecture_notes.pdf so the compare endpoint can detect shared claims about parser routing, retrieval, OCR quality, and study-guide generation.
1. Shared topic with the English PDF
2. Component mapping table 3. Query scenarios table
4. Risk and mitigation table 5. Expected compare behavior

AgentBook is a retrieval-augmented learning system that ingests heterogeneous study materials and exposes endpoints for ask, summarize, compare, study-guide, and graph-based question answering. This document mirrors the PDF topic but adds additional tables so that table retrieval can be tested more directly.

citation.snippet_original: The retrieval pipeline begins with material normalization. Text blocks, table blocks, figure captions, OCR blocks, and audio transcript segments are converted into a common schema. This schema preserves the source page, sheet, timestamp, block type, and confidence score when applicable. The normalized schema is essential because downstream agents should not care whether a fact came from a PDF paragraph or a spreadsheet row.

evidence_blocks.snippet_original: The retrieval pipeline begins with material normalization. Text blocks, table blocks, figure captions, OCR blocks, and audio transcript segments are converted into a common schema. This schema preserves the source page, sheet, timestamp, block type, and confidence score when applicable. The normalized schema is essential because downstream agents should not care whether a fact came from a PDF paragraph or a spreadsheet row.

comparison_table.value: {'intuition': 'Trong cách hiểu, việc xử lý tài liệu giống như một quá trình chuyển đổi từ nhiều dạng dữ liệu khác nhau thành một cấu trúc chung. Điều này giúp hệ thống có thể hiểu và sử dụng thông tin từ nhiều nguồn khác nhau mà không cần biết về nguồn gốc của nó.'}

citation.snippet_original: A correct answer should cite the relevant block, avoid unsupported claims, and preserve the answer language requested by the user.

evidence_blocks.snippet_original: A correct answer should cite the relevant block, avoid unsupported claims, and preserve the answer language requested by the user.
Question: What debug signal confirms that vectors were written?
Question: How does the system support compare across two documents?
Question: What is the role of the vector indexer? Question: Which endpoint can create a study guide? Question: Why should the system reject low-quality OCR images?

comparison_table.value: {'intuition': 'Noelys dựa trên tài liệu để hiểu cách hệ thống xử lý các loại tài liệu khác nhau. Nó giúp xác định các phương pháp mà hệ thống sử dụng để giải quyết các yêu cầu từ người dùng, như việc phân tích và trả lời câu hỏi từ tài liệu học tập.'}

citation.snippet_original:
| Module | Input type | Block type expected | Test question |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Docling parser | PDF/DOCX/PPTX | text/table/figure | Which parser handles text-based PDF? |
| EasyOCR fallback | Scanned PDF and image | ocr_text | What should happen when the scan is readable? |
| SpreadsheetParser | CSV/XLSX | table | Which product has the highest price? |
| AudioParser | MP3/WAV | audio_segment | What is the speaker talking about? |

evidence_blocks.snippet_original:
| Module | Input type | Block type expected | Test question |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Docling parser | PDF/DOCX/PPTX | text/table/figure | Which parser handles text-based PDF? |
| EasyOCR fallback | Scanned PDF and image | ocr_text | What should happen when the scan is readable? |
| SpreadsheetParser | CSV/XLSX | table | Which product has the highest price? |
| AudioParser | MP3/WAV | audio_segment | What is the speaker talking about? |

| Endpoint | Required material | Expected output |
| --- | --- | --- |
| POST /query/ask | lecture_notes.pdf | Vietnamese answer grounded in English document |
| POST /query/compare | lecture_notes.pdf + comparison_doc.docx | Shared claims and differences |
| POST /query/study-guide | any indexed PDF | Learning objectives, key concepts, quiz questions |
| GET /materials/{id}/debug | any material | chunk_count and vector count |

| Risk | Trigger file | Severity | Mitigation |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| OCR hallucination | scan_low_quality.png | High | Reject low-quality input and expose error message |
| Table chunking error | workbook_multi.xlsx | Medium | Preserve sheet name and row context |
| Figure caption missing | slides_mixed.pptx | Medium | Generate figure captions and store figure blocks |
| Cross-lingual retrieval drop | lecture_notes.pdf | Medium | Use multilingual embeddings or query translation |

citation.snippet_original: A correct answer should cite the relevant block, avoid unsupported claims, and preserve the answer language requested by the user.

evidence_blocks.snippet_original: A correct answer should cite the relevant block, avoid unsupported claims, and preserve the answer language requested by the user.
Question: What debug signal confirms that vectors were written?
Question: How does the system support compare across two documents?
Question: What is the role of the vector indexer? Question: Which endpoint can create a study guide? Question: Why should the system reject low-quality OCR images?

comparison_table.value: {'example': 'Trong trích đoạn, ví dụ được đưa ra là việc chuyển đổi giữa các loại tài liệu như PDF, DOCX thành một cấu trúc chung[1]. Ví dụ này cho thấy cách hệ thống xử lý và hiểu thông tin từ nhiều nguồn khác nhau.'}

citation.snippet_original: The summarize endpoint should produce a concise overview of the material, including system purpose, input modalities, parser behavior, and debug signals. The study-guide endpoint should be more pedagogical: it should list learning objectives, define key terms, propose review questions, and highlight practical implementation risks such as low-quality OCR images or incorrectly chunked tables.

evidence_blocks.snippet_original: The summarize endpoint should produce a concise overview of the material, including system purpose, input modalities, parser behavior, and debug signals. The study-guide endpoint should be more pedagogical: it should list learning objectives, define key terms, propose review questions, and highlight practical implementation risks such as low-quality OCR images or incorrectly chunked tables.

The most important evaluation rule is that a parser should fail loudly when the input is too poor to process. Silent failure is worse than refusal because it allows hallucinated answers to appear trustworthy. For this reason, the test pack includes both pass and fail examples.
Table 2. Evaluation plan for the AgentBook sample pack.

comparison_table.value: {'example': "[1] mô tả cách tài liệu này được tạo ra để so sánh với tài liệu 'lecture_notes.pdf'. Nó cũng cung cấp thông tin về các module xử lý tài liệu, bao gồm cách hệ thống xử lý dữ liệu từ nhiều nguồn khác nhau và cách nó tương tác với người dùng."}

evidence_blocks.snippet_original: Comparison Document for AgentBook Parser Tests
Purpose: this DOCX is intentionally aligned with lecture_notes.pdf so the compare endpoint can detect shared claims about parser routing, retrieval, OCR quality, and study-guide generation.
1. Shared topic with the English PDF
2. Component mapping table 3. Query scenarios table
4. Risk and mitigation table 5. Expected compare behavior

AgentBook is a retrieval-augmented learning system that ingests heterogeneous study materials and exposes endpoints for ask, summarize, compare, study-guide, and graph-based question answering. This document mirrors the PDF topic but adds additional tables so that table retrieval can be tested more directly.

citation.snippet_original: The summarize endpoint should produce a concise overview of the material, including system purpose, input modalities, parser behavior, and debug signals. The study-guide endpoint should be more pedagogical: it should list learning objectives, define key terms, propose review questions, and highlight practical implementation risks such as low-quality OCR images or incorrectly chunked tables.

evidence_blocks.snippet_original: The summarize endpoint should produce a concise overview of the material, including system purpose, input modalities, parser behavior, and debug signals. The study-guide endpoint should be more pedagogical: it should list learning objectives, define key terms, propose review questions, and highlight practical implementation risks such as low-quality OCR images or incorrectly chunked tables.

The most important evaluation rule is that a parser should fail loudly when the input is too poor to process. Silent failure is worse than refusal because it allows hallucinated answers to appear trustworthy. For this reason, the test pack includes both pass and fail examples.
Table 2. Evaluation plan for the AgentBook sample pack.

comparison_table.value: {'limitation': 'Một hạn chế của phương pháp này là nó không thể xử lý các tài liệu phức tạp hoặc có chứa thông tin không rõ ràng, đặc biệt là khi dữ liệu gốc bị hỏng hoặc không rõ ràng. Đồng thời, việc sử dụng quá nhiều nguồn dữ liệu khác nhau cũng có thể làm tăng độ phức tạp và khó hiểu cho hệ thống.'}

citation.snippet_original: AgentBook ingests materials from PDF, DOCX, PPTX, image, spreadsheet, and audio sources. Each material is assigned to a collection so that experiments can isolate ordinary text retrieval from OCR-heavy and table-heavy workflows. The system stores both raw block metadata and vector representations, enabling semantic search as well as debugging through material-level diagnostics.

evidence_blocks.snippet_original: AgentBook ingests materials from PDF, DOCX, PPTX, image, spreadsheet, and audio sources. Each material is assigned to a collection so that experiments can isolate ordinary text retrieval from OCR-heavy and table-heavy workflows. The system stores both raw block metadata and vector representations, enabling semantic search as well as debugging through material-level diagnostics.

comparison_table.value: {'limitation': '[4] chỉ ra một số rủi ro có thể xảy ra trong quá trình xử lý tài liệu. Tuy nhiên, không có thông tin cụ thể về các giải pháp khắc phục hoặc cách hệ thống xử lý những rủi ro này.'}
